analysis_summary: The proposed method produced 8 finding(s) from 8 analysis obligation(s).

findings.summary: Top-level does not visibly gate DMI/JTAG requests to dm_top with jtag_unlock or another authorization signal, but dmi_jtag internals were not provided, so the absence of authentication cannot be proven from this scope alone.
findings.vulnerability_category: DMI read/write requests, debug_req trigger, ndmreset/dmactive control through debug module / dm_top debug controls and state: debug_req_o, ndmreset_o, dmactive_o, dm_slave_* and dm_master/debug NoC-facing interfaces / permission-related RTL security issue
findings.reasoning_summary: Subject: external JTAG/debug requester via tck_i/tms_i/td_i/trst_ni, or simulation DTM/JTAG requester under compile-time simulation paths Operation: DMI read/write requests, debug_req trigger, ndmreset/dmactive control through debug module Object: dm_top debug controls and state: debug_req_o, ndmreset_o, dmactive_o, dm_slave_* and dm_master/debug NoC-facing interfaces Expected guard: debug authentication/authorization, lifecycle/test-mode restriction, or explicit jtag_unlock/auth check before DMI requests can reach dm_top and cause debug entry/reset/debug-visible access Observed behavior: riscv_peripherals connects dmi_jtag outputs debug_req/debug_req_valid directly to dm_top DMI inputs. dmi_jtag receives hash inputs and produces jtag_unlock, but no top-level expression gates debug_req_valid/debug_req with jtag_unlock. testmode_i is passed into dmi_jtag and dm_top but no top-level authorization predicate is visible. Debug NoC bridge connects to dm_master; dm system-bus response signals are tied off. Path: tck_i/tms_i/trst_ni/td_i -> tck/tms/trst_n/tdi assignments -> i_dmi_jtag -> debug_req/debug_req_valid/debug_resp -> dm_top DMI ports -> debug_req_o/ndmreset_o/dmactive_o and debug AXI-lite/NoC interface Closure verdict: Related RTL evidence exists, but some core evidence slots remain incomplete.
findings.security_impact: If dmi_jtag does not internally authenticate before asserting DMI valid, external JTAG can reach debug-module operations and potentially trigger debug entry/reset or access debug state.
findings.uncertainty_or_missing_evidence: dmi_jtag internals and dm_top authorization behavior were not included; cannot determine whether authentication is enforced inside dmi_jtag.

findings.summary: Access-control matrix coverage appears incomplete: bootrom and many wrapper interfaces are mediated by acc_ctrl_c, but PLIC/CLINT/debug request paths are not observed to be gated by acc_ctrl_c/reglk_ctrl.
findings.vulnerability_category: read/write/configure through NoC/MMIO request paths / bootrom, CLINT/PLIC, crypto/security wrappers AES/RSA/RNG/SHA/HMAC, PKT, ACCT, REGLK, DMA and their control/status registers / permission-related RTL security issue
findings.reasoning_summary: Subject: CPU/NoC/MMIO requester represented by peripheral NoC inputs and priv_lvl_i Operation: read/write/configure through NoC/MMIO request paths Object: bootrom, CLINT/PLIC, crypto/security wrappers AES/RSA/RNG/SHA/HMAC, PKT, ACCT, REGLK, DMA and their control/status registers Expected guard: all NoC/MMIO requests to protected peripherals should be gated by acc_ctrl_c[priv_lvl_i][peripheral] and applicable reglk_ctrl lock bits at actual read/write enable sites Observed behavior: acc_ctrl_c is generated and used for bootrom and numerous wrapper acct_ctrl_i ports. Search evidence found acc_ctrl_c use at wrapper instantiations and bootrom, but no CLINT/PLIC acc_ctrl_c use. PLIC state machine asserts plic_req.valid from AXI valid/ready without visible acc_ctrl_c or reglk_ctrl condition. Debug NoC path feeds dm_master without acc_ctrl_c. Path: NoC2 inputs -> noc_axilite_bridge instances -> peripheral AXI/req interfaces; acc_ctrl from acct_wrapper -> acc_ctrl_c[priv_lvl_i][peripheral] -> selected wrapper acct_ctrl_i or rom_req; PLIC/CLINT/debug paths bypass observed matrix gating Closure verdict: All required core evidence slots are present for a candidate violation.
findings.security_impact: MMIO requesters may access PLIC/CLINT/debug-related paths without the same privilege-matrix enforcement applied to bootrom and security wrappers.
findings.uncertainty_or_missing_evidence: Full CLINT code was not shown; PLIC evidence is explicit for the inspected state-machine excerpt. External address filtering, if any, is not evidenced.

findings.summary: reglk_wrapper lock state is cleared whenever jtag_unlock is asserted, and top-level connects jtag_unlock directly from dmi_jtag without an additional lifecycle/authorization guard at the clear site.
findings.vulnerability_category: write, clear, unlock, or change register-lock policy / reglk_ctrl_o / reglk_ctrl policy bits controlling protected peripheral registers / permission-related RTL security issue
findings.reasoning_summary: Subject: MMIO/AXI requester configuring reglk_wrapper and JTAG/debug unlock subject producing jtag_unlock Operation: write, clear, unlock, or change register-lock policy Object: reglk_ctrl_o / reglk_ctrl policy bits controlling protected peripheral registers Expected guard: register-lock configuration should be writable only before lock or under authenticated jtag_unlock; once set, lock bits should be sticky or cleared only by reset/lifecycle-authorized condition Observed behavior: reglk_ctrl_o is built from reglk_mem. The sequential reset branch in reglk_wrapper uses if (~(rst_ni && ~jtag_unlock && ~rst_9)), clearing all reglk_mem entries when global reset is active, rst_9 is active, or jtag_unlock is asserted. Normal writes under en && we update whole reglk_mem words based on selected reglk_ctrl_i bits. Top-level passes jtag_unlock from dmi_jtag directly to reglk_wrapper. Path: JTAG/hash path -> dmi_jtag.jtag_unlock_o -> riscv_peripherals jtag_unlock -> reglk_wrapper.jtag_unlock -> reset branch clearing reglk_mem -> reglk_ctrl_o -> top-level reglk_ctrl slices used by protected wrappers Closure verdict: All required core evidence slots are present for a candidate violation.
findings.security_impact: If jtag_unlock can be asserted by an insufficiently authenticated debug/JTAG path, all register locks can be cleared, enabling later changes to access policy and protected peripheral registers.
findings.uncertainty_or_missing_evidence: dmi_jtag authentication strength for jtag_unlock is outside provided evidence.

findings.summary: Access-control policy registers are software-visible through AXI/NoC and reset to permissive all-ones; writes are gated by acct_ctrl_i and selected reglk_ctrl bits, but no independent privileged configuration authority is evidenced at the write use site, and we_flag can force policy bits high.
findings.vulnerability_category: write/configure access policy / acc_ctrl_o / acc_ctrl policy matrix controlling peripheral access by privilege / permission-related RTL security issue
findings.reasoning_summary: Subject: AXI/MMIO requester configuring access-control policy Operation: write/configure access policy Object: acc_ctrl_o / acc_ctrl policy matrix controlling peripheral access by privilege Expected guard: access-control policy registers should be writable only by an authorized/privileged configuration subject and protected against writes when corresponding reglk_ctrl lock bits are set Observed behavior: acct_wrapper exposes AXI-lite writes. en is en_acct && acct_ctrl_i, where top-level drives acct_ctrl_i from acc_ctrl_c[priv_lvl_i][6]. On reset/rst_6, acct_mem entries become 32'hffffffff. acc_ctrl_o concatenates acct_mem words and ORs acct_mem[0] with {8{we_flag}}, allowing external we_flag_0 to force bits high. Writes under en && we update acct_mem words unless selected reglk_ctrl bits block them. Path: NoC acct port -> noc_axilite_bridge -> acct_axi_req -> acct_wrapper AXI-lite interface -> en/we/address/wdata -> acct_mem -> acc_ctrl_o -> riscv_peripherals acc_ctrl -> acc_ctrl_c -> peripheral acct_ctrl_i/request gates Closure verdict: All required core evidence slots are present for a candidate violation.
findings.security_impact: A requester already permitted to access ACCT can rewrite policy to loosen access controls; reset defaults are permissive; external we_flag can bypass stored policy by forcing allow bits.
findings.uncertainty_or_missing_evidence: Truncated acct_wrapper content omitted later addresses/read side; no separate privilege ID beyond priv_lvl_i-derived acct_ctrl_i is shown.

findings.summary: pkt_wrapper permits software-visible fuse requests and fuse data readback when acct_ctrl_i allows the wrapper; fuse_req_o and fuse_addr_o writes are not directly guarded by reglk_ctrl, and read logic can expose fuse_rdata_i at address 4 when reglk_ctrl_i[6] is clear.
findings.vulnerability_category: request/read/expose fuse or key-location data / fuse_req_o, fuse_addr_o, fuse_rdata_i and derived pkey_loc/read data / permission-related RTL security issue
findings.reasoning_summary: Subject: software-visible AXI/MMIO requester reaching packet/fuse wrapper Operation: request/read/expose fuse or key-location data Object: fuse_req_o, fuse_addr_o, fuse_rdata_i and derived pkey_loc/read data Expected guard: fuse read requests and returned fuse data should be mediated by privilege/access-control and lock/lifecycle policy; sensitive fuse contents should not be exposed on ordinary read data without authorization Observed behavior: pkt_wrapper gates AXI activity by en = en_acct && acct_ctrl_i. On writes, address 0 sets fuse_req_o from wdata[0] and address 1 sets fuse_addr_o from wdata, with no reglk_ctrl_i check at the write site. Read path returns pkey_loc halves unless reglk_ctrl_i[4]/[5] are set and returns fuse_rdata_i at case address 4 unless reglk_ctrl_i[6] is set. It also initializes rdata to fuse_rdata_i whenever en before case selection. pkt instance is driven with req_i(1'b1) and fuse_indx_i(fuse_addr_o). Top-level connects fuse_mem outputs and JTAG hashes to the same fuse memory source. Path: NoC pkt port -> noc_axilite_bridge -> pkt_axi_req -> pkt_wrapper en/we/address/wdata -> fuse_req_o/fuse_addr_o -> top-level fuse_mem -> fuse_rdata_i -> pkt_wrapper rdata -> AXI/NoC response Closure verdict: All required core evidence slots are present for a candidate violation.
findings.security_impact: Authorized or mis-authorized access to PKT can trigger arbitrary fuse reads and read fuse-derived data; if acc_ctrl/reglk policy is permissive or mutable, sensitive fuse contents/key-location data may be exposed over NoC.
findings.uncertainty_or_missing_evidence: Full top-level pkt instantiation line range before 1690 is partially truncated, but signals and wrapper behavior are explicit.

findings.summary: DMA configuration registers and PMP inputs are present, but provided evidence is truncated before the complete write guards, active-transfer behavior, range checks, and DMA master/target path; cannot confirm violation or satisfaction.
findings.vulnerability_category: configure/start DMA read/write transfer / DMA configuration registers start_reg, length_reg, source_addr_*_reg, dest_addr_*_reg, core_lock_reg, valid/done/end state and DMA memory access path / permission-related RTL security issue
findings.reasoning_summary: Subject: software-visible AXI/MMIO requester and DMA engine as bus master Operation: configure/start DMA read/write transfer Object: DMA configuration registers start_reg, length_reg, source_addr_*_reg, dest_addr_*_reg, core_lock_reg, valid/done/end state and DMA memory access path Expected guard: DMA configuration and start should be permitted only to authorized subjects; source/destination address ranges should be checked against access policy/PMP or equivalent, and config updates should be blocked while active/locked Observed behavior: dma_wrapper exposes AXI-lite interface and gates en as en_acct && acct_ctrl_i. It declares PMP inputs pmpcfg_i/pmpaddr_i, reglk_ctrl_i, acct_ctrl_i, we_flag, reset rst_8, and configuration/status registers. Reset branch begins by clearing core_lock_reg/start/length/source/dest/done/end/valid, but the evidence is truncated before showing write cases, range/PMP checks, or downstream DMA transfer interface. Path: NoC DMA port -> noc_axilite_bridge -> dma_axi_req -> dma_wrapper AXI-lite interface -> configuration registers -> unknown downstream DMA transaction path Closure verdict: Related RTL evidence exists, but some core evidence slots remain incomplete.
findings.security_impact: Potential DMA authorization/range bypass cannot be adjudicated from available evidence.
findings.uncertainty_or_missing_evidence: Complete dma_wrapper write cases, range/PMP checking logic, active-transfer locking, and bus-master datapath were not present in the observations.

findings.summary: Software/NoC-visible peripheral reset controls appear security-sensitive because wrapper reset inputs rst_5/rst_6/rst_8/rst_9 clear fuse request/address, access policy, DMA config, and register locks; reset generation authorization is not evidenced, and rst_9 can clear lock policy.
findings.vulnerability_category: trigger reset/clear security state / peripheral reset signals rst_1..rst_13 and reset-sensitive policy/secret/control registers / permission-related RTL security issue
findings.reasoning_summary: Subject: software/NoC requester or debug reset requester Operation: trigger reset/clear security state Object: peripheral reset signals rst_1..rst_13 and reset-sensitive policy/secret/control registers Expected guard: software-triggered or NoC-visible reset controls for security-relevant peripherals should require privilege/access-control/lifecycle/debug authorization and should not clear locks/policies except under intended reset authority Observed behavior: Top-level declares rst_1..rst_13 and has a reset NoC interface. Wrappers consume rst_5/rst_6/rst_8/rst_9. pkt_wrapper reset clears fuse_req_o/fuse_addr_o; acct_wrapper reset/rst_6 sets acct_mem to 32'hffffffff (permissive policy); dma_wrapper reset/rst_8 clears DMA config/status registers; reglk_wrapper reset/rst_9 clears reglk_mem lock policy to zero. Evidence of reset-control write/decode authorization is not provided. Path: NoC reset port -> rst_axi_req/rst logic (not fully shown) -> rst_1..rst_13 -> wrapper reset inputs rst_5/rst_6/rst_8/rst_9 -> protected/security state cleared or made permissive Closure verdict: All required core evidence slots are present for a candidate violation.
findings.security_impact: If peripheral resets are software-triggerable without strong authorization, an requester can clear locks/policies or force permissive defaults, affecting later access control and state integrity.
findings.uncertainty_or_missing_evidence: Reset-control address decode and authorization logic around rst_* generation was not provided.

findings.summary: Crypto/security NoC read paths are routed through wrappers that receive acc_ctrl_c and reglk_ctrl slices, but wrapper internals and read muxes for AES/RSA/RNG/SHA/HMAC were not provided; no direct key/intermediate-state exposure can be confirmed.
findings.vulnerability_category: read/expose cryptographic or random/secret state / NoC response data for AES0/AES1/AES2/RSA/RNG/SHA256/HMAC and wrapper read mux data such as ariane_*_buf_noc3_data_o / permission-related RTL security issue
findings.reasoning_summary: Subject: NoC/MMIO requester reading security peripherals Operation: read/expose cryptographic or random/secret state Object: NoC response data for AES0/AES1/AES2/RSA/RNG/SHA256/HMAC and wrapper read mux data such as ariane_*_buf_noc3_data_o Expected guard: readback from crypto/security peripherals should expose only intended status/results and be mediated by access-control/lock; internal keys/intermediate state should not be directly routed to NoC/debug read paths Observed behavior: Top-level instantiates crypto/security wrappers and NoC AXI-lite bridges. RSA and HMAC excerpts show wrapper acct_ctrl_i from acc_ctrl_c[priv_lvl_i][index], reglk_ctrl_i slices, debug_mode_i, and AXI response r.data routed to NoC3 data through the bridge. Search results show analogous acc_ctrl_c connections for AES/RNG/SHA/HMAC/RSA. The actual wrapper read mux/data classification is not in provided files. Path: NoC2 crypto/security request ports -> noc_axilite_bridge -> wrapper AXI req -> wrapper read response data -> noc_axilite_bridge -> ariane_*_buf_noc3_data_o/valid_o Closure verdict: Related RTL evidence exists, but some core evidence slots remain incomplete.
findings.security_impact: Potential exposure of keys/intermediate/stale crypto state cannot be ruled out, but the available evidence only proves the existence of access-controlled wrapper connections, not unsafe readback.
findings.uncertainty_or_missing_evidence: AES/RSA/RNG/SHA/HMAC wrapper read muxes, internal key/state classifications, and debug_mode_i effects are not included.

global_uncertainty: No closure-level missing evidence was recorded beyond the visible source scope limitations.
